paragraphs.text: I wish to support the idea of improving the digital market by granting more right to customers. I would like to as well suggest to take actions against dark patterns, illegal terms of service, and as well against the companies that shut down a service, or the access to a digital product, right after your purchase without any prior notice. This proble affact as well physical products, since new products rely on a cloud service, and without it most of they can't work or lose key features. There is as well the urge to address the awful practice to prevent clients from writing review, if they didn't spend a minimum amount of money on the platform. This practice is done by Steam, but on the other hand we have Google that deny the possibility to being a verified app developer unless you pay. We had streaming and gaming platforms shutting down services and sometimes entire libraries of purchases products... This shouldn't happen, we should be able to download our products and get notified in order to can download them before their removal. I'm personally against privacy invasion, I don't like when sites collect my datas and I wish you avoid implementing things such as digital ID, because they can be dangerous if any of the databases that stores such informations get hacked... They are very sensitive datas and something like this already happened in USA, compromising even bank accounts... Another problem consist on digital censorship applied by payament processors and big monopolies, which shouldn't be able to deny our purchases as long as they are legal. In addition to this problem, majority of parents let their kids alone with digital devices, but then they cry when they stole their money... Companies shouldn't be punished because of bad parenting... This issue need to be fixed punishing the parents and involving social services. Internet doesn't need more invasive ways to verify identities just because some parents are lazy, a kid can't make a bank account by himself, it's the parents fault if the kid use their money. This open the problem of in game monetization, which isn't really a problem, because only adults can choose to open a bank account and own a credit card. Going back to dark patterns, an example of them is found in the new game Blue Protocol: Star Resonance. It is less a game and more an outdated, visually incoherent shop masquerading as an open-world MMORPG. Its "open world" feels like a desolate corridor with unrewarding exploration. Graphically, it's a mess, featuring flat, poorly modeled characters overlaid on hyperrealistic, clashing backgrounds. Core progression is a time-gated daily chore tied solely to the main story, making mob-killing and grinding pointless. The game is aggressively monetized, incorporating a chaotic mix of gacha systems and paywalls for everything, from cosmetics to vital resources, all built using confusing currencies and manipulative dark patterns to push excessive spending. This game features multiple passes, as well a monthly card and as I mentioned before it features multiple kinds of currencies, some of them are not even labeled as currency, but they come in form of tickets, materials and so on, but you can convert them into other currencies, which makes the game and its economy really confusing and misleading. Finding a way to farm for free it's hard thanks to multiple menus inside other menus, which combined to the lack of tutorials and the time-gate on several free activites make the game unplayable without paying, because paying let you get essential items to improve, while farming is limited because of the in game timers... But the problem is as well that several items are locked behind a gacha system, so you need luck and thanks to the various currencies it's hard to tell how much you are spending... The total experience is boring, confusing, and feels like a low-effort cash-grab. We need clear rules and clear ways to keep track of our money.
